# Initial Protocol Design: Distributed Autonomous Agent Fleet Protocol (DAAFP) v1.0
This protocol commits to a decentralized, peer-to-peer framework for AI agent fleets, with explicit, standardized workflows for discovery, trust, and coordination. It prioritizes resilience, security, and fairness for resource-constrained autonomous agents (e.g., drones, warehouse robots, autonomous vehicles).

---

### Weakest Unvalidated Assumption in Initial DAAFP v1.0
The single most fragile, untested foundational assumption is:
> **All peer discovery advertisements (local mDNS broadcasts and cross-subnet Kademlia responses) are sent by agents that cryptographically prove they own the private key corresponding to the AID they advertise.**
